attributes.action: Recognise extended clinical academic training in NHS consultant pay progression
attributes.additional_details:
Doctors undertaking academic training spend additional years contributing to UK research while remaining on trainee salaries. 

Under current NHS rules this time does not count towards consultant salary seniority, creating a financial penalty. 

Estimated lifetime losses are £70-80k per year of research.

At a time when the UK is seeking to strengthen its life sciences sector and reverse declining numbers of medical academics, pay arrangements should support -not discourage- academic careers.
attributes.background: Review NHS consultant pay progression rules so that formally recognised clinical academic training undertaken during specialty training (such as a PhD, MD or Academic Clinical Lectureship) can count towards consultant salary seniority, as other recognised extensions to training already do.
